original_hypothesis.title: Timelock schedule/execute can be effectively immediate for securityCouncil due to getMinDelay override (governance delay bypass)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TaikoTimelockController.getMinDelay returns 0 for TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE holders. Deployment scripts explicitly grant TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE to the securityCouncil and also show schedule+execute with delay=0 patterns. This enables same-transaction governance actions by the admin role, bypassing the intended 7-day timelock delay and enabling instant upgrades/address changes if the admin role is abused or compromised.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete governance bypass present in code and reinforced by deployment practice. While exploitation requires privileged access (low realism under the strict on-chain-only standard), the operational impact is protocol-wide and immediate.
deployment_assumptions: TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE is granted to externally controlled accounts (e.g., securityCouncil) that can initiate timelock operations
Timelock is actually used to guard upgrades/address changes in production
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol grants TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE to securityCouncil and uses schedule(..., 0) then execute(..., 0) patterns; TaikoTimelockController.getMinDelay returns 0 for TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE callers.
preconditions: Attacker controls an account with TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE (or can obtain it through governance/ops failure)
Sensitive operations are callable through timelock (upgradeTo, AddressManager.setAddress, etc.)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Admin schedules and executes sensitive operations with zero effective delay.

operational_questions: Which accounts hold TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE on mainnet deployments?
Are there monitoring and out-of-band controls preventing immediate admin actions?
Is the admin bypass intentionally relied on for emergency response, and if so, what compensating controls exist?
validation_requirements: Replay schedule/execute flows and confirm zero-delay execution for admins

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Use TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE account to schedule an operation with delay=0.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Execute immediately (same block/tx sequence) due to getMinDelay override.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Perform upgrade/address remap to attacker-controlled implementation, then drain/brick dependent components.

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Full protocol control via upgrades/address mapping
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Immediate hostile upgrade or dependency swap (bridge/signal_service/verifiers)
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High; may require governance recovery or social intervention

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Zero-delay timelock executions by admin role
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Unexpected upgrades or address changes without prior delay
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Admin operations clustered without notice period

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
